originalPrompt: Framework: Express, Styling: Tailwind CSS. Request: create hello world app, where I can modify the text "hello world" colors through a frontend UI.

subtasks.title: Initialize project and install dependencies
subtasks.description: Create a new Node project, then install Express, Tailwind CSS, PostCSS, and Autoprefixer. Ensure a clean folder structure for server and static assets.
subtasks.successCriteria: package.json exists and npm install completes with express, tailwindcss, postcss, and autoprefixer listed as dependencies or devDependencies.

subtasks.title: Configure Tailwind CSS build pipeline
subtasks.description: Initialize Tailwind with PostCSS, set proper content paths, create input CSS with @tailwind directives, and add npm scripts for building and watching CSS output to public/styles.css.
subtasks.successCriteria: Running npm run build:css generates public/styles.css containing Tailwind utilities without errors.

subtasks.title: Implement Express server and static hosting
subtasks.description: Create server.js to serve static files from a public directory and respond to the root path with index.html. Add an npm start script to launch the server.
subtasks.successCriteria: npm start launches an Express server that serves files from /public and returns index.html at GET /.

subtasks.title: Build frontend page with color controls
subtasks.description: Create public/index.html using Tailwind for layout and styles. Add a visible 'Hello World' element and UI controls (e.g., color input and preset buttons). Add public/app.js to update the text color on user interaction.
subtasks.successCriteria: Loading the page shows a styled 'Hello World' and controls; changing a control updates the text color immediately without reload.

subtasks.title: Build assets, run server, and verify UI behavior
subtasks.description: Execute the Tailwind build, start the Express server, and test the UI in a browser to confirm that color changes apply and no console errors occur.
subtasks.successCriteria: public/styles.css is generated, server runs on the chosen port, and the 'Hello World' color changes via UI controls in the browser.

decompositionStrategy: Top-down setup: initialize dependencies, configure Tailwind build, stand up Express static server, implement UI controls, then verify end-to-end.
